title: papaver dubium var. luteorubrum
intro: Papaver dubium var. luteorubrum, also known as the golden poppy, is a variant of the wild poppy plant, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers and delicate, papery petals. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anticonvulsant, and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Papaver dubium var. luteorubrum include its potential to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity and interactions with medications.
